scope: brand-new 10-page triple-perspective trilingual audit, seed 20260525, disjoint from all prior samples

findings.defect: 安全性 translated as 'Safety' instead of 'Security'. The row description explicitly cites 'Unauthorized Access' and 'acts of destruction' (破壊行為) — these are security concepts, not safety concepts. In IT audit terminology, 'Safety' refers to operational/functional safety (IEC 61508 domain), whereas 'Security' refers to protection from malicious or unauthorized acts. A learner anchoring to 'Safety' will map this to the wrong domain on the exam.
findings.evidence_excerpt: jp: '安全性', en: 'Safety', row description en: 'Degree of protection of information systems from natural disasters, Unauthorized Access, and acts of destruction'

findings.defect: Page 127 contains only a figure caption with no accompanying term entity. The caption 'Penalty rather than reward' (報酬よりも、罰金) references a concept (implicitly loss aversion / prospect theory) without any definitional scaffolding on the same page. A learner navigating by page has no textual anchor for this concept.
findings.evidence_excerpt: leaf_count: 1, single entity type: figure, caption: 'Penalty rather than reward'

findings.defect: Colloquial register in formal definition: 'If this type of problem appears in the exam, consider yourself lucky.' The JP source has 'ラッキーです' and ZH has '就赚到了' — all three are colloquial. While internally consistent, informal expressions in a structured term definition reduce pedagogical formality and may appear out of place in export formats targeting formal study materials.
findings.evidence_excerpt: definition.en: '...If this type of problem appears in the exam, consider yourself lucky...'

findings.defect: Asymmetric acronym expansion across languages: ZH surface is '信息技术基础架构库（ITIL）' (full expansion + acronym), but JP surface is bare 'ITIL' and EN surface is bare 'ITIL'. A learner reading JP or EN surfaces will not see the expansion. Acronym-expansion-before-use rule requires consistent treatment. Either all three surfaces should carry the expansion, or the expansion should appear in the definition uniformly.
findings.evidence_excerpt: surface.jp: 'ITIL', surface.zh: '信息技术基础架构库（ITIL）', surface.en: 'ITIL'

findings.defect: EN definition of 'Best Practice' states 'Means "best examples."' The standard IT meaning of 'best practice' is a recommended methodology or approach recognized as superior — not merely 'examples.' The JP '最良の事例' can mean 'best cases/examples' but the EN rendering should preserve the technical meaning: 'best practice = an approach recognized as most effective.' Translating as 'best examples' conflates practice with example and may mislead learners on exam questions.
findings.evidence_excerpt: definition.en: 'Means "best examples." In the IT Passport exam, when "best practice" appears, it is almost certainly a question about ITIL.'

findings.defect: Inconsistent EN rendering of the boolean JP 'する' within the same table column 'Data display on the client side': Client-Server System row uses 'Yes', while Web System row uses 'Perform' and Thin Client row uses 'Perform'. JP source uses 'する' uniformly for all three. The mixed Yes/Perform mapping breaks parallel structure within the table, creating confusion for learners comparing rows. ZH also shows '进行' vs '执行' inconsistency for the same cell type.
findings.evidence_excerpt: Client-Server/display: jp='する', zh='进行', en='Yes'; Web/display: jp='する', zh='执行', en='Perform'; Thin/display: jp='する', zh='执行', en='Perform'

findings.defect: Grid Computing described as achieving 'high-speed processing comparable to a supercomputer' — this is a simplified/marketing claim. Grid computing achieves high aggregate throughput via distributed parallelism but differs from a supercomputer in latency, coherence, and architecture. Additionally, the contrast 'cloud servers concentrated in one location vs. grid scattered worldwide' is an oversimplification; modern cloud spans multiple regions. Acceptable for IT Passport entry-level but may embed misconceptions.
findings.evidence_excerpt: definition.en: 'A system that achieves high-speed processing comparable to a supercomputer by connecting multiple computers via the Internet. Whereas the servers in Cloud Computing are concentrated in one location, in Grid Computing the computers serving as servers are scattered around the world.'

findings.defect: ESSIDステルス (entity index 0) is defined before ESSID itself (entity index 1) on the same page. The acronym-expansion-before-use rule requires that ESSID be introduced and expanded before it is used in a compound term. A learner reading entities sequentially encounters 'ESSID Stealth' with the definition referencing 'ESSID' before that term has been defined.
findings.evidence_excerpt: p480::0 surface.en: 'ESSID Stealth', definition references 'ESSID'; p480::1 surface.en: 'ESSID (Extended Service Set IDentifier)' — defined after

findings.defect: Inconsistent PDCA expansion pattern in EN PDCA column: 'P (Plan)' and 'C (Check)' have no colon-expansion of the English word, while 'D (Do: Execute)' and 'A (Act: Action)' add an expansion after a colon. This inconsistency within the same column creates non-parallel formatting. Additionally, the content cells expand ISMS as '(Information Security Management System)' in D and C rows but not in P and A rows — a second inconsistency.
findings.evidence_excerpt: rows: 'P (Plan)' vs 'D (Do: Execute)' vs 'C (Check)' vs 'A (Act: Action)'; content D: 'Introduction and Operation of ISMS (Information Security Management System)', content A: 'Maintenance and improvement of ISMS'

findings.defect: Non-standard capitalization in EN surface: 'ESSID (Extended Service Set IDentifier)' — 'IDentifier' uses mid-word uppercase 'D' which is non-standard typography. Standard rendering is 'Identifier'.

Redundant acronym expansion in ZH definition: '利用PoE（以太网供电）' expands PoE again within the definition body, despite the ZH surface already providing '以太网供电（PoE）'. This double-expansion is harmless but cosmetically redundant.
findings.evidence_excerpt: surface.zh: '以太网供电（PoE）', definition.zh: '利用PoE（以太网供电）...'
